regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather remains generally stable with moderate east to east-southeast trade winds. San Juan reports 74°F with light easterly winds at 7 knots and a 35% chance of scattered showers today, increasing to a 55% rain chance tomorrow as winds build to 16 knots. Expect seas near 3-5 feet, with no Small Craft Advisories in effect across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, or Turks & Caicos. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor localized showers and plan for gusty conditions tomorrow, especially near open anchorages; passengers should carry light rain gear and expect brief wet spells during daytime hours.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean weather for March 15, 2026, is generally mild with temperatures near 79°F and southeast winds 7 knots. Cozumel and nearby ports will see mostly overcast skies today with minimal rain risk, but expect increasing shower chances by Day 1, especially around Costa Maya and Belize City where scattered rain showers may impact visibility and deck operations. Winds strengthen to 12 knots from the SSE through Day 2, then shift NNE at 18 knots with a 78% chance of rain showers on Day 3, raising seas and reducing anchorage comfort in exposed harbors like Roatan and Grand Cayman. Captains should monitor gear and plan reduced fishing or tender operations during heavier showers; passengers advised to carry rain gear and anticipate some outdoor activity interruptions.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region today features mild conditions with light ESE winds 7 knots and overcast skies, minimal rain chance at 1%. Expect increasing ESE to SE winds up to 15 knots over the next three days, accompanied by intermittent drizzle and rising rain chances—up to 68% by day three. Mariners should monitor Freeport and Nassau anchorages for reduced visibility and slick decks due to drizzle; small craft operators should remain vigilant as winds approach Small Craft Advisory thresholds later in the week. Passengers should plan for light rain gear and limited outdoor activities during periods of drizzle, especially near Turks & Caicos.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean weather for March 15, 2026: Aruba reports 79°F with east winds at 17 knots under partly cloudy skies and an 18% chance of rain. Expect gradual wind increases with a Small Craft Advisory likely by Day 2 as easterly winds reach 22 knots. Rain showers are scattered today through Day 1, decreasing thereafter; conditions in Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena will be similar with light rain chances and steady easterly trades. Mariners should ensure lines and fenders are secure in all ports, especially in Aruba and Curacao where choppier seas may challenge anchorage; fishing captains should plan for moderate winds and brief showers early today but improving conditions by midweek. Passengers should carry light rain gear for occasional showers.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today feature light ESE winds at 8 knots, temperatures near 79°F, and partly cloudy skies with minimal rain risk. Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Cozumel, and Costa Maya can expect generally stable weather through tomorrow with winds increasing to 13-14 knots and isolated showers up to 28 percent chance. By March 17, thunderstorms with an 82 percent rain chance will develop near Grand Cayman, warranting caution for small craft and fishing operations—secure loose gear and monitor updated forecasts closely. Anchorage conditions remain favorable today, but captains should prepare for deteriorating visibility and rougher seas later in the period.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles ports will experience breezy ENE winds at 20-22 knots with frequent rain showers today, especially near Barbados, where rain chances reach 80%. Seas will remain choppy; Small Craft Advisories are advised for fishing and yacht operations, particularly around open anchorages in St. Lucia and Martinique. Expect improving conditions with partly cloudy skies and lower rain chances by March 16-17, but maintain vigilance for localized showers that may impact visibility. Passengers should plan for indoor activities during peak rain periods and mariners should secure loose gear and monitor updated forecasts.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and surrounding ports (Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, Catalina Island) will see light southerly winds today at about 4 knots with persistent fog and no rain. Daytime temperatures will rise to the low 70s under foggy conditions, increasing to mid-80s by Tuesday and Wednesday with a shift to westerly winds and overcast skies late Wednesday. Mariners should expect limited visibility near shore, especially early and late in the day; exercise caution entering and leaving harbors. Cruise and fishing captains should plan for smooth seas and light winds but remain vigilant for fog-related navigation challenges around Catalina Island and Marina del Rey anchorages.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions today feature cool temperatures near 57°F with light easterly winds at 1 knot and overcast skies; no precipitation expected. Visibility may be reduced early due to lingering fog, especially near Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos, so mariners should maintain vigilant navigation in these areas. Over the next three days, winds will shift to the southwest at 7 knots with warming temperatures reaching up to 87°F by day three; fog will persist through day two, impacting morning operations. Yacht and fishing captains should plan for calm seas and minimal wind stress but remain cautious in fog-prone anchorages, while cruise passengers should prepare for variable visibility during morning hours, improving by afternoon.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports including San Jose del Cabo and La Paz will see stable, clear conditions today with light northwest winds at 12 knots and temperatures near 74 degrees Fahrenheit. Winds will increase slightly over the next two days but remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, with clear to partly cloudy skies and no rain expected. Mariners can expect calm seas and good visibility for all operations; fishing captains should note steady northwest winds favoring offshore trips. Anchorage in the Cabo San Lucas harbor will remain protected with no adverse weather impacts anticipated.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region conditions for March 15 show mild temperatures near 75-76°F with northwest to west winds 6-10 knots and no precipitation expected. Skies will alternate between clear and overcast, but visibility remains good for navigation and fishing. Mariners can expect calm seas with no Small Craft Advisories or warnings. Port operations in Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata remain normal; anchorage conditions are stable with light winds and minimal swell. Cruise passengers should enjoy outdoor activities with light layers recommended for cooler mornings and evenings.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is experiencing calm, clear conditions this morning with light northeast winds at 2 knots and temperatures near 78°F. Expect a gentle west to southwest breeze increasing to 7-8 knots over the next three days with partly cloudy to overcast skies but no rain. Mariners should find smooth seas and excellent visibility across Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala harbors, ideal for fishing and yacht operations. Cruise passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with minimal weather disruption; captains should maintain routine watch but no advisories are in effect.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 15, 2026: Winds across key ports including Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar remain moderate from the northwest to west at 4-8 knots with clear to partly cloudy skies and no precipitation expected through the next 72 hours. Daytime temperatures will rise into the low to mid 60s Fahrenheit, providing comfortable conditions for cruise passengers and leisure craft operations. Mariners should expect calm seas with no Small Craft Advisories or gale warnings in effect; anchorage conditions in Barcelona and Palma de Mallorca remain stable with good holding in moderate winds. Fishing captains can plan for steady conditions, but maintain vigilance for local sea breezes developing in the afternoon that may cause light shifts in wind direction nearshore.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean outlook for March 15, 2026: Naples reports mild conditions with 56°F, northeast winds at 4 knots, and overcast skies, with minimal rain chance today. Expect light easterly winds near 7 knots and a 20% chance of rain today, increasing to intermittent rain and northeast winds around 10-12 knots on Days 2 and 3. Mariners should monitor Genoa and Civitavecchia for similar overcast and light rain conditions, with no Small Craft Advisories at present. Passengers should carry light rain gear and plan for occasional showers, while yacht and fishing captains should verify anchorage security in exposed harbors like Venice and Dubrovnik ahead of moderate northeast winds.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean weather for March 15, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) reports mild conditions with clear skies, light WSW winds at 2 knots, and no precipitation expected today. Over the next three days, expect overcast skies with light ESE to NE winds 4-10 knots, patchy fog developing on day two, and a slight chance of rain increasing to 15% by day three. Mariners should note generally calm seas and no Small Craft Advisories; however, fishing captains should exercise caution navigating nearshore in foggy areas around Mykonos and Rhodes on day two. Cruise passengers should prepare for overcast conditions and limited visibility near Istanbul and Izmir later in the week.
